After many years of opposition
the labor unions of Wallace and vi
entity have been able to organize
a good strong central labor coun-
Gil, chartered under the A. f. of
L., and every union is affiliated
with the exception of the BARRIERS.
The miners union is one of the
most important factors in the new
body.


The success in maintaining the
union spirit and organization in
the Occur d'Alenes on the part of
union men is more remarked when
the determined attempts made by
the mine owners to crush out every
spark of unionism in that district
is considered.


That the support given union la
Dor of every description by the
men in the Occur d'Alenes Is of
the right kind is shown in the num-
Der of cigarmakers in Wallace.
Notwithstanding that large guam
titles of cigars manufactured in
this city are sold in the Occur
d'Alenes there are today as many
union cigarmakers employed In
Wallace as there are in Spokane
Practically all the cigars sold In
the big mining district are union
made, and thousands Of dollars
which would be sent out of the
country is kept in circulation In
the Idaho mining district.
